frames: The agent begins lying on its side, then pushes its lower limbs against the ground to lift its body.
It continues to rise, bending its lower limbs to bring its main body segment into a more vertical orientation.
The agent achieves an upright, crouched posture, balancing on its bent lower limbs with its upper body also flexed.
It holds this stable, crouched position, with its lower limbs firmly planted and a slight, controlled sway in its upper body.
The agent's upper body sways gently from side to side, while its bent lower limbs maintain continuous ground contact.
It continues to exhibit subtle balancing movements, keeping its core upright and its limbs flexed.
The agent's upper segment leans slightly forward, and its lower limbs make minor adjustments to maintain stability.
It concludes in a stable, crouched stance, with all limbs bent and planted, appearing ready to initiate locomotion.
